cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what Steve Rowson thinks of the newer football helmets. The options are A to D. First, I need to find where in the text Steve Rowson is mentioned. The text says: "Steve Rowson is an engineer at Virginia Tech. In his lab, he tests how helmets hold up against different types of hits. No helmet can prevent concussions completely, says Rowson. But he's found that when wearing a newer football helmet, the player's head doesn't gain speed as much after a hit. That lowers the risk of concussions." So, what does this mean? The key part here is "the player's head doesn't gain speed as much after a hit" which means the newer helmets reduce the acceleration of the head upon impact. This reduction in speed would lower the risk of concussions according to Rowson. The question is about what Rowson thinks of the new helmets. The options are about size variation, whether they work better than old ones, whether they slow down speed, or allow more free movement. Looking at the options: A) They vary a lot in size. The text doesn't mention anything about size variation in the newer helmets. So probably not A. B) They work better than the old ones. The text says that the new helmets lower the risk because the head doesn't gain speed as much. This implies that they are more effective than older helmets. So B could be correct. C) They slow down the player's running speed. The text says the player's head doesn't gain speed as much after a hit. It's about the head's acceleration not the player's running speed. So C might be a trick option because it's about the head's movement, not the player's overall speed. So C is incorrect. D) They allow the players to move their heads more freely.
